advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Give a concrete unique naming convention for `<branch>`/`<path>` and a fallback if `git worktree add` fails.
evidence: examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md:31: "`git fetch origin && git worktree add <path> -b <branch> origin/main`"
ABSENCE: no guidance on choosing a non-colliding `<branch>`/`<path>`, nor what to do if the command fails.
advisory.finding: `git worktree add <path> -b <branch> origin/main` fails when `<branch>` already exists (or when not in a git repo / path is occupied), so the mandated 'very first step of skill execution' can hard-fail with no naming convention or fallback given.
scenarios: A branch with the chosen placeholder name already exists.
Re-running the skill after a prior run left a worktree/branch behind.
advisory.suggested_fix: Suggest a unique name (e.g. include the ticket id or a timestamp) and note the command must not collide with an existing branch/path.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Note that abandoned/crashed runs may leave a worktree and how to clean up stragglers (e.g. periodic `git worktree prune` / list).
evidence: examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md:33-36: 'When the skill concludes ... retire the worktree with `git worktree remove <path>` (then `git worktree prune`), so it does not leak'
advisory.finding: Teardown is described as firing 'when the skill concludes — whether the fix lands, the run fails, or you abandon it', but an agent that errors out or is interrupted before reaching that step will still leak the worktree; the doc frames leak-prevention as guaranteed.
scenarios: The agent crashes or is interrupted mid-run.

coaching.coaching: Update the user/operator/API docs that track the worktree setup command's failure modes.
coaching.subject: the worktree setup command's failure modes

coaching.coaching: Update the user/operator/API docs that track worktree teardown on interruption or error.
coaching.subject: worktree teardown on interruption or error
